{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for the treatment of choice in childhood thyrotoxicosis. From the retrieved documents, particularly Document [0], it is stated that children with thyrotoxicosis may be treated with methimazole, radioiodine therapy, or thyroidectomy. Methimazole is specifically noted as the first-line therapy for Graves disease in children, typically administered for 1 to 2 years, with some children achieving remission. Radioiodine therapy is not recommended for children aged 5 or younger. Surgery is mentioned as a definitive treatment option, especially in severe cases or when antithyroid drugs are ineffective or contraindicated, but it is not described as the first-line or preferred choice in children. Lugol's iodine is mentioned in the context of preoperative preparation to reduce gland vascularity and is not presented as a primary treatment for thyrotoxicosis in children. Among the options, Carbimazole is equivalent to methimazole (a common name for methimazole), and the documents refer to methimazole as the first-line therapy in pediatric patients. Therefore, Carbimazole (option C) is the most appropriate treatment of choice in childhood thyrotoxicosis based on the evidence.", "answer_choice": "C"}
